objective: Independently review the complete remediated HCM-0.1 control pack, retest HCM-R-F-001 through HCM-R-F-006 and HCM-R2-F-001, and close HCM-0.1 only if every normal and negative proof is clean.

Record schema-version routing and ledger parity

work_completed: Read the complete HCM-0.1 control pack, current schemas/template/validator/history/ledger, both reviews and remediations, selected architecture-memo sections, and dispatch-named live Rust/core assets without modifying any reviewed artifact.

Revalidated live fixed artifact/layout and Markdown-canonical Charter precursor truth, confirmed the target artifact-kind/intake/posture/Context Resolution/Snapshot types remain absent, and preserved every runtime/open-program gate and seam label.

Retested HCM-R-F-001 through HCM-R-F-006 and HCM-R2-F-001 across current cross-document authority and mechanical validators, including exact historical v1.0 hash admission and independent malformed-history scenarios.

Kept HCM-0.1 open because the Phase 2 renderer versus Phase 3 Projection contract remains cross-document inconsistent and the validator accepts deletion of an admitted immutable historical v1.0 record after an exact ledger rebuild.
Made no remediation, Rust, test, core asset, schema, template, validator, dispatch, or skill change; wrote only this review handoff and its matching ledger entry.

deliverables.summary: Immutable v1.1 different-fresh final review record with two actionable findings and an HCM-0.1 open verdict.

decisions.summary: Live runtime truth remains fixed artifact/layout and Markdown-canonical Charter precursor behavior; target artifact-kind/intake/posture/Context Resolution/Snapshot seams remain unimplemented.
decisions.authority_effect: No Rust is authorized, every runtime/open program proof gate stays open, and no seam classification is promoted.

decisions.summary: HCM-R-F-001, HCM-R-F-003, HCM-R-F-004, HCM-R-F-005, and HCM-R-F-006 retest cleanly, and the exact HCM-R2 unknown/byte-modified v1.0 admission cases now fail closed; HCM-R-F-002 and immutable-history deletion protection do not close cleanly.
decisions.authority_effect: The completed-clean-review condition is unsatisfied, so HCM-0.1 remains open for bounded docs/control-infrastructure remediation and a later different fresh review.

findings.summary: HCM-R-F-002 is not fully resolved. Phase 2 is now required to use fixed deterministic non-Resolution renderers, but 01 and 05 still call Markdown/Charter views projections, HCM-2.1 explicitly requires a derived Markdown projection, and the universal Projection definition/provenance rules require a Resolution envelope. The pack therefore does not say whether a Phase 2 review renderer is outside the Phase 3 Projection contract or must prematurely carry Resolution/projection provenance. Bounded repair: consistently classify Phase 2 outputs as renderer-derived views outside the Phase 3 Projection contract, or define an explicit non-Resolution renderer provenance subset and narrow every universal Projection statement accordingly.

The historical-v1.0 validator protects additions and byte modification but not deletion. In an isolated copy, deleting one admitted canonical v1.0 record and rebuilding ledger.jsonl exactly made normal validation pass with 12 records/12 entries. The admission mapping is consulted only for records still present; validation never requires all seven admitted immutable filenames to exist. This permits canonical history truncation while schema, parity, and byte-rebuild proof remain green. Bounded repair: require the canonical record set to contain every exact admitted v1.0 filename/ID/hash entry and add a deterministic deletion negative test without changing immutable records.

blockers.summary: HCM-0.1 cannot close while the Phase 2 renderer/Phase 3 Projection authority boundary remains internally inconsistent.
blockers.resume_condition: A separate bounded docs remediation aligns 01/02/04/05 and a later different fresh reviewer returns clean.

blockers.summary: HCM-0.1 cannot close while the canonical validator permits deletion of admitted immutable historical v1.0 history after an exact ledger rebuild.
blockers.resume_condition: A separate bounded validator/protocol proof repair rejects deletion while all exact current records and other negative cases remain green, followed by a different fresh review.

Define whether Phase 2 renderer outputs are outside the Phase 3 Projection contract or have a separate non-Resolution provenance subset.

pack_updates.reason: Remove the HCM-2.1 Markdown-projection contradiction while retaining Phase 3 sequencing.
Keep Phase 2 renderer outputs distinct from the Resolution-envelope request/result contract.
Reject deletion of any exact admitted immutable v1.0 record after a rebuilt ledger.
Require preservation/deletion refusal for the exact seven admitted v1.0 canonical records if not already explicit.
State and prove that immutable admitted history cannot be removed as well as not being modified.

next_session.objective: Select this review, validate both findings, and dispatch one bounded docs/control-infrastructure remediation; after repair, dispatch another different fresh independent complete HCM-0.1 review.

sections: Phase 3 — Vocabulary, Context Resolution, Snapshot Memory, and projections

allowed_scope: Orchestrate the smallest docs-only alignment of the Phase 2 renderer versus Phase 3 Projection contract across the exact named sections.
Orchestrate the smallest validator/protocol negative proof that rejects deletion of any exact admitted historical v1.0 record.
Require full normal, existing negative, new deletion-negative, links/portability/archive, docs-only, diff, and read-only GitNexus proof, then a different fresh complete review.
prohibited_scope: Do not rewrite immutable handoff records or prior dispatches.
Do not change either handoff schema/template unless a new bounded finding proves it necessary.
Do not implement Rust, modify tests/core assets/installed skills, select shipped defaults, or start later slices.
Do not close HCM-0.1 from remediation alone and do not use this reviewer or the remediation agent as the next independent reviewer.
acceptance_criteria: Phase 2 renderer outputs and the Phase 3 Resolution-aware Projection contract have one unambiguous cross-document boundary.
All seven current historical v1.0 records remain byte-identical and valid, and unknown, byte-modified, or deleted admitted v1.0 history fails deterministically.
The full normal validator, every required negative scenario, and all docs/control proof checks pass after closeout.
A later different fresh independent reviewer reports no unresolved actionable finding before HCM-0.1 closes.
stop_conditions: Stop if repair would rewrite immutable history, select defaults, authorize Rust, or widen into Phase 0.2/0.3 semantic freeze work.
Keep HCM-0.1 open until both bounded repairs and another different fresh complete review are clean.

chat_summary: Different fresh final review leaves HCM-0.1 open. Phase 2 non-Resolution renderers still conflict with universal Projection/Resolution provenance language, and exact-ledger validation permits deletion of admitted immutable v1.0 history. Known unknown/modified-v1.0 negatives pass; no Rust/runtime gate or seam changed.
